stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv A B) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (A :: Γ) B
priority_rationale: A full left-division residuation equivalence is a central structural bridge from sequent rules to reusable characterization, but at this stage it is still one core correspondence rather than a summary-rewriting theorem family by itself.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv B A) ↔ Γ ≠ [] ∧ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ [A]) B
priority_rationale: The right-division analogue is equally central: it would expose the ordered-context behavior of the calculus and give a reusable criterion for many later derivations, but it remains a local-to-structural bridge rather than a frontier-closing result.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ A → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.tp_degree A ≤ Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.list_degree Γ
priority_rationale: A degree bound would provide a reusable invariant linking derivability to structural complexity, which is meaningful given the agenda around rule reducibility and complexity, even though it is not yet a full characterization theorem.
